Article Title: Will Nikola Jokic stay with Denver Nuggets? The Serbian superstar’s unexpected move sparks concerns
Article Text:
Nikola Jokic. Image via: Justin Edmonds/ Getty Images

Will Nikola Jokic stay with the Denver Nuggets?



Statistic



2025-26 Season



Points per game



27.7



Rebounds per game



12.9



Assists per game



10.7



Efficiency Rating



37.7



NBA Rebounding Rank



1st



NBA Assists Rank



1st





Why Nikola Jokic's decision is rattling for the Denver Nuggets?



Nikola Jokic of Denver Nuggets (Image via AP Photo)

Nikola Jokic's salary cap and contract implications



Nikola Jokic (Image via USA TODAY Sports)

The question of Nikola Jokic staying with the Denver Nuggets has resurfaced after reports indicated the three-time NBA MVP is once again leaning toward postponing contract extension talks despite becoming eligible for a massive new deal this offseason. The decision does not necessarily signal an intention to leave Denver, but it does increase pressure on the franchise to prove it can build another championship-caliber roster around its cornerstone player.According to NBA insiders Marc Stein and Jake Fischer , Jokic is once again considering delaying extension negotiations with the Nuggets."Jokic informed the Nuggets around this time last year that he preferred to delay contract extension talks for another year," Stein and Fischer noted."The Stein Line has learned that it is under consideration again this summer: Electing to take the same step and holding off on extension talks."The report mirrors what occurred a year ago, when Jokic also declined to immediately negotiate a new deal. At the time, the move made financial sense because waiting allowed him to qualify for a significantly richer extension.Jokic is eligible to sign a four-year contract extension worth approximately $278 million following the conclusion of the 2025-26 season.Even if he waits another year, league rules allow him to pursue essentially the same maximum contract, giving him little financial incentive to rush into an agreement.Jokic has repeatedly expressed his desire to remain in Denver, and there is no indication that he has requested a trade or is preparing to leave.Still, delaying an extension naturally creates uncertainty for a franchise whose championship hopes revolve almost entirely around the Serbian center.The Nuggets understand that Jokic has earned the opportunity to evaluate the team's direction before committing several more years to the organization.Jokic averaged a triple-double for the second straight season while once again finishing among the league's MVP finalists. Few players in NBA history have maintained that level of consistency while carrying such a significant offensive workload.Because of that, many around the league view the latest contract decision less as a warning sign and more as a message to Denver's front office.Financially, the Nuggets remain in a strong position.Jokic is under contract through next season and also holds a player option for the 2027-28 campaign. This summer, he is eligible to sign an extension worth approximately four years and $278 million, depending on how his player option is handled.If he waits until next offseason, he can still negotiate essentially the same maximum extension, eliminating any urgency from his perspective.For Denver, though, every month without a new agreement increases the importance of roster construction.The organization has already indicated it is pursuing trades to improve the supporting cast around Jokic while carefully managing its payroll. Decisions involving veterans, young contributors and future draft assets could ultimately shape not only the 2026-27 season but also whether the franchise secures another long-term commitment from the player who transformed it into an NBA champion.The Nuggets still possess one of basketball's greatest competitive advantages as long as Jokic remains in uniform. The next several months will reveal whether Denver's offseason moves provide enough evidence that another championship window remains wide open before extension talks eventually return to the table.
Article Summary: Nikola Jokic is eligible to sign a four-year contract extension worth approximately $278 million. The decision does not necessarily signal an intention to leave Denver. But it does increase pressure on the franchise to prove it can build another championship-caliber roster.

Article Title: Tyrese Haliburton injury update: Indiana Pacers star drops promising news amid Achilles recovery
Article Text:
Tyrese Haliburton. Image via: Jacob Kupferman/ Getty Images

Tyrese Haliburton gives a major recovery update after Achilles rehabilitation



Tyrese Haliburton of the Indiana Pacers (Image via Getty)

Haliburton's recovery gives Indiana Pacers reason for optimism entering next season



Indiana Pacers’ coach Rick Carlisle drops crucial injury update on key player Tyrese Haliburton (Image Source: Getty)

After a disappointing Indiana Fever year, Tyrese Haliburton is set to make his return to the court in the upcoming season. Haliburton’s injury update brought encouraging news for the Indiana Pacers on Saturday. The franchise point guard said he feels fully healthy more than a year after suffering a torn Achilles tendon in Game 7 of the 2025 NBA Finals.Speaking while hosting his annual youth basketball camp in Indianapolis, Haliburton expressed confidence that he will be ready for the start of the 2026-27 NBA season after missing all of last year during his recovery.Indiana reached the NBA Finals behind its All-Star guard before his devastating injury against the Oklahoma City Thunder ended both his season and the team's championship hopes. Now, with more than three months remaining before training camp, Haliburton believes he is approaching full basketball condition.Haliburton did not hold back when describing where he stands physically after spending the past year recovering from one of basketball's most difficult injuries."My body feels great," Haliburton said. "I feel great moving. I feel great playing. My body just feels great in all honesty.I've never felt this strong. I still gotta get in a little bit of shape but I feel great."It's exciting and it's only what, the end of June here. I still have three more months. I feel like I'm in good shape, NBA shape, but there's a difference between NBA shape and Tyrese Haliburton shape. I'll get there."Haliburton's absence defined Indiana's 2025-26 campaign. After leading the Pacers to their first NBA Finals appearance since 2000, he watched from the sidelines while completing a lengthy rehabilitation program following the Achilles rupture that occurred during the deciding game of the championship series.The guard said the recovery process also included overcoming another unexpected health challenge. A bout with shingles earlier this year affected his appearance and temporarily forced him to gain weight."I look back at videos of me and I can see how swollen my face is," Haliburton said. "I'm glad that's beyond me and past me now. I feel great. My body feels great. I'm able to do everything."I feel like I'm operating like I'm a healthy NBA player for the first time in a long time, which is exciting. The fact that I'm able to come here without having to answer questions from anybody in our organization about my health, that I'm just able to jump in and do some things with the kids, that means the world to me. It's something I don't take for granted after the last 12 months."Saturday's appearance came during Haliburton's annual basketball camp, where more than 300 children in grades one through eight participated in skill stations alongside several members of the Pacers organization. Forward Obi Toppin, guard Taelon Peter, newly drafted Braden Smith, and general manager Chad Buchanan all joined the event, underscoring the franchise's support for one of its cornerstone community initiatives.With training camp still months away, Indiana does not need to rush its franchise point guard. Haliburton's latest update instead shifts attention toward how quickly he can rediscover his All-NBA form once competitive games return. If his recovery continues on its current path, the Pacers could enter the 2026-27 season with the player who led them to the brink of a championship finally back on the floor.
Article Summary: Tyrese Haliburton suffered a torn Achilles tendon in Game 7 of the 2025 NBA Finals. The Indiana Pacers franchise point guard said he feels fully healthy more than a year after the injury. With more than three months remaining before training camp,Haliburton believes he is approaching full basketball condition. The guard said the recovery process also included overcoming another unexpected health challenge.

Article Title: Mike Vrabel's ex-Patriots teammate silently likes post hinting at unanswered questions in Dianna Russini scandal
Article Text:
Mike Vrabel & Dianna Russini (Getty Images)

What are the unanswered questions in the Dianna Russini–Mike Vrabel NYT report?

The Dianna Russini–Mike Vrabel story just refuses to go away. Tony Farmer, host of the "Last Men Standing" podcast, posted on X asking what people made of former Patriots linebacker Ted Johnson apparently liking his article listing five new questions raised since The New York Times' bombshell report. Johnson won three Super Bowls as Vrabel's teammate between 2001 and 2004, which makes the quiet endorsement all the more pointed. It is a small act, but in a story built on what people knew and when, it speaks volumes.Farmer's five questions dug into specifics the NYT report left dangling: who was the NFL head coach Dianna Russini allegedly phoned to get out of a texting-while-driving ticket, what exactly did her "unofficial advisor" role during Mike Vrabel's coaching job search look like, did that relationship colour her reporting, and why did The Athletic open a review of her involvement in the January 2025 feature "Inside Mike Vrabel's Year Off. "There is also the question of The Athletic's own editors. Farmer raised why executive editor Steven Ginsberg publicly defended Russini after the first Sedona images surfaced, despite apparently not having seen all the photos at that point. Ginsberg had initially told staff the images "lacked essential context," but later acknowledged that "as additional information emerged, new questions were raised that became part of our investigation.Farmer's central argument is that this relationship was hiding in plain sight. He pointed to NYT reporting that members of Vrabel's coaching staff were present with the two at a casino, writing on X: "It's getting harder to believe execs at ESPN and The Athletic didn't know about the friendship because it seems like Vrabel and Russini weren't trying very hard to hide it. These execs won't answer questions. Is it possible they not only knew, but they encouraged it?"That is a serious charge. If leadership at two major sports media organisations knew about the closeness between a top NFL reporter and an active head coach and stayed silent, the ethical questions extend well beyond Russini herself.Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic before the internal investigation concluded and never disclosed any conflict of interest to editors. In a text exchange with a NYT reporter, she described herself as a "former journalist" and asked not to be quoted, unaware the correspondence was on the record. The NFL confirmed it is not reviewing Vrabel's conduct under its personal conduct policy. Vrabel, meanwhile, has continued to prepare for the 2026 season without major interruption. Russini remains off the air with no confirmed return.Ted Johnson has not explained the like. He does not need to. In a story where almost nobody in a position of authority is talking, even a double-tap carries weight.
Article Summary: The Dianna Russini–Mike Vrabel story just refuses to go away. Tony Farmer, host of the "Last Men Standing" podcast, posted on X asking what people made of former Patriots linebacker Ted Johnson apparently liking his article listing five new questions raised since the NYT report.

Article Title: "Believe it or not": Will Levis' ex Gia Duddy drops a bombshell response to Druski dating rumors
Article Text:
Gia Duddy and Druski Dating Rumors (Instagram and Getty Images)

Was Gia Duddy really in the car with Druski?

Who is Gia Duddy dating now?

Social media influencer Gia Duddy has firmly denied claims that she was the curly-haired woman spotted in comedian Druski's SUV after a night out in Los Angeles with Travis and Jason Kelce. A blurry TMZ photo from outside the Bird Streets Club in West Hollywood circulated widely, and X users quickly concluded the passenger in Druski's Cadillac Escalade was Duddy. She was not amused by the speculation and went straight to TikTok to shut it down.No. And she made that very clear.In a TikTok video posted Thursday, Duddy addressed the rumours with the kind of directness most people reserve for correcting family at the dinner table. "I have a really quick little PSA for everybody," she told her followers. "Believe it or not, there are more than one women in this world with really beautiful curly hair and that the woman in the photo with Druski was not me."She did not stop there. She joked about the assumption while still rejecting it: "I know, she had great hair. Everyone would just obviously guess that is Gia Duddy." The self-awareness was clear. She knows how fame works -- a blurry photo, a recognisable hairstyle, and the internet fills in the rest.Duddy confirmed she had been back home in Pennsylvania for about ten days at the time the photo was taken, spending time with her boyfriend and their dog. That detail alone made the Druski rumor geographically impossible. "It was not me. It is not me. I was back home in Pennsylvania. I've never met Druski. Don't know the bro. Seems like a funny dude, but don't know him," she said in the video.Duddy confirmed she is in a relationship, though she stopped short of naming her partner. She said her boyfriend is "100 percent not Druski," which may be the most specific denial she was willing to offer. The most prominent theory among social media sleuths points to Cincinnati Bengals wide receiver Andrei Iosivas, whose ex-girlfriend appeared to break up with him earlier this year. Duddy had posted a TikTok about flowers that fans connected to a Cincinnati florist Iosivas is known to support, though neither has confirmed the relationship publicly.Duddy first became a household name during the 2023 NFL Draft, when cameras caught her by Will Levis's side through an agonizing evening that stretched well past the first round without his name being called. That night turned her into one of the most searched people on the internet overnight. Since then, she has built a sizeable following on her own terms.She closed her TikTok video the way she started it -- without drama and with a bit of humor. "Wasn't me. Just clearing the air. But she did have really great hair and I get why you guys would like obviously guess it was me. But it wasn't."
Article Summary: Gia Duddy denies she was the curly-haired woman spotted in comedian Druski's SUV. A blurry TMZ photo from outside the Bird Streets Club in West Hollywood circulated widely. Duddy confirmed she is in a relationship, though she stopped short of naming her partner.

Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma and MS Dhoni (Image credit: Agencies)

Wedding celebrations in Bihar and Varanasi

NEW DELHI: India fast bowler Akash Deep recently began a new chapter in his personal life, marrying Akshita Raj in a traditional ceremony in Varanasi. The cricketer shared pictures and videos from the wedding on social media on Wednesday. One clip from the celebrations quickly caught the attention of fans because of Akash's humorous response to a question about some of Indian cricket's biggest stars.During the event, a guest asked him why former India captain MS Dhoni and star batters Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma were not invited to the wedding.Akash responded with a smile: "Shaadi ho paayega yahan Banaras mein?" (Will the wedding even take place here in Banaras? )His answer drew laughter from those present and suggested that the presence of such high-profile cricketers could have attracted huge crowds and made it difficult to conduct the ceremony smoothly.Ahead of the wedding, celebrations had already begun in Akash's native village of Baddi in Bihar's Rohtas district. Family members, friends and well-wishers gathered for traditional functions before the ceremony in Varanasi.The wedding marked a happy occasion for the pacer, who has spent the past few months recovering from injury.Akash has represented India in 10 Test matches and taken 28 wickets. His best innings figures are 6 for 60, while his best match figures are 10 for 112.One of the standout moments of his career came during India's tour of England last year, when he claimed 12 wickets in the Birmingham Test against England.The right-arm pacer was signed by Kolkata Knight Riders for IPL 2026 but missed the entire season because of a lower-back stress injury.The injury also kept him out of India's plans for the one-off Test against Afghanistan earlier this month.Akash last played for India during the England tour last year, with his most recent appearance coming in the fifth Test at The Oval. Article Title: What’s next after Starmer: A lowdown on the making of the UK's next PM
Article Text:
Britain has no written constitution. So, what happens now? A lowdown on making of Britain’s next PM

What comes next is a feature of British political life dominated by conventions: the leadership election.

The author is a Senior Resident Fellow at the Vidhi Centre for Legal Policy.

When Margaret Thatcher was deposed by rebel MPs 1990, she deemed it “treachery with a smile on its face”. Indeed, world leaders were shocked at the inexplicable machinations of the Westminster machine. Soviet leader Gorbachev, in the middle of a politburo meeting when he heard the news, sent out his foreign minister, Eduard Shevardnadze, to find out if the inconceivable had really happened. Shevardnadze returned with confirmation, remarking that five years earlier, Soviet Union had party coups, and the British had elections – and now, it was the other way around. Henry Kissinger in Washington compared the loss of Thatcher to a “death in the family”. Divisive as she may have been, the iron lady stood as a giantess on the world stage. Such anguished paeans are absent in the case of the politically late Keir Starmer.What comes next is a feature of British political life dominated by conventions: the leadership election. Britain, which does not have a codified constitution, important constitutional moments are governed by constitutional conventions – rules, written, unwritten, ancient, and modern, which determine how the rat race is to be run.The first, and most important, conventional requirement is that the Prime Minister, and the leader of a party seeking to govern, must be a member of the lower house of parliament. It is the satisfaction of this requirement which explains, at least in part, Starmer’s decision to resign. Until late last week, Andy Burnham, the top contender to replace Starmer, was not a member of the House, instead serving as the popular Mayor of Manchester. With Burnham parachuting into Westminster via a by-election, he became eligible to replace the beleaguered premier.In the past, promising candidates have lost out on the chance to lead their party (or the country) owing to this convention. Tony Benn, the firebrand leader of the left wing of Labour Party in the 1980s, missed his best chance at leadership of the party as he was ineligible to stand, having lost his parliamentary seat at the 1983 general election.Unlike in India, modern British PMs may not, by convention, sit from the upper house of parliament – the House of Lords. Twice in the past century, this de facto disqualification was thrust upon promising leaders. In 1923, Stanley Baldwin was chosen as the Conservative Party leader (and hence Prime Minister) over Lord Curzon (of Bengal fame) as it was, on balance, considered unwise to have a titled aristocrat, unanswerable to the lower house, as Prime Minister. Four decades later, in 1963, when the Earl of Home was chosen as the leader of the governing Conservative Party, it was considered totally unacceptable. Sir Alec Douglas-Home, as he subsequently became, then disclaimed his peerage (gave up his hereditary title) and stood for byelection to the lower house to legitimise his claim to the premiership.Rajya Sabha being an indirectly elected rather than a hereditary or nominated House, there is no such bar to legitimacy in India. Four Indian PMs: Indira Gandhi (1966 to 1967); Deve Gowda; IK Gujral; and Manmohan Singh have held that office from Rajya Sabha. The last British PM to sit from the Lords was the Marquess of Salisbury, who held the office from 1895 to 1902.The second convention that must be followed is that the PM must demonstrate to the King that he or she has the confidence of his parliamentary party. Starmer cited the loss of this confidence as a reason for his exit. Burnham will now have to demonstrate his. Indeed, in the past, PMs have used resignation as a threat to whip their parties into line. In 1995, John Major famously resigned, daring the internal critics of his European policy to “put up or shut up” and to challenge him openly in a leadership contest. Except for a token contest from the Oxford don and Welsh Secretary John Redwood, they did neither. But the gambit allowed Major to remain in post for another 2 years.Labour Party’s rules now state that in order to get on the leadership ballot, a candidate must show support from 20% of the party’s MPs (that is, 81 MPs) and 5% of all the constituency-level Labour Party groups in the country or at least 3 of the ‘affiliated’ organisations of the Labour Party (mostly the larger trades unions). Every candidate who clears these hurdles is put to a vote of all party members in a ranked-choice voting system, with the winner being crowned leader.The final convention is, of course, appointment by the King. Once internal party formalities are complete, the new leader must go to Buckingham Palace to ‘kiss hands’ and accept formal appointment as the monarch’s first minister. Of course, the new PM’s real work – an anaemic economy, an especially troubled special relationship, and a resurgent right wing – will only begin after this grand processional, with all its pomp and circumstance, is complete.
Article Summary: The author is a Senior Resident Fellow at the Vidhi Centre for Legal Policy. Britain does not have a codified constitution, important constitutional moments are governed by constitutional conventions. The first, and most important, conventional requirement is that the leader of a party seeking to govern, must be a member of the lower house of parliament.

Article Title: 'America, meet your next federal budget director’: JD Vance jokes after Usha Vance buys $50 dress for $8.75
Article Text:
JD Vance and his wife, Usha Vance, discuss her $8.75 Old Navy dress

Poll Vote & Share your view Should political figures use personal life moments, like pregnancy, to promote political messages? Yes, it's relatable. No, it undermines serious issues. It depends on the situation. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

US Vice President JD Vance seized on his wife's bargain maternity dress to score a political point on federal spending, after US Second Lady Usha Vance mocked a New York Times column that analysed the "political symbolism" of her pregnancy wardrobe.In a post on X on Thursday, Vance shared a photo of a receipt showing his wife had bought a $50 Old Navy maternity dress for $8.75 and wrote: "America: meet your next director of the federal budget!"The comment followed Usha Vance's own swipe at a NYT fashion column by critic Vanessa Friedman titled "The Politics and Power of the Pregnancy Image," which examined how three prominent women in the Trump administration, the Second Lady, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt, and Katie Miller, wife of Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller, had publicly showcased their pregnancies at roughly the same time.Friedman argued the pattern was deliberate, writing that the women had "created a notably consistent, and somewhat paradigm-shifting, picture of the White House's family and fertility platform." She added that Usha Vance, by spotlighting her pregnancy, was doing her job of humanising the Vice President.Usha Vance sharply responded to Friedman’s piece: "Now that we know the political significance of my $8.75 coral maternity dress from Old Navy, can't wait to hear what the New York Times has to say about my elastic-waistband pants and compression socks," she wrote on X, attaching the receipt.JD Vance's federal budget quip framed the exchange as a broader statement on fiscal restraint that fits squarely into the administration's working-class economic messaging. With household costs remaining a top concern for American voters, the optics of a Vice President's wife buying an $8 dress, rather than designer maternity wear, reinforce an everyman image the administration has been trying to cultivate.Ironically, JD Vance has been one of the Trump administration's most vocal champions of American manufacturing, accusing previous administrations of deciding "America would no longer be a manufacturing power" and letting "the rest of the world make the necessary things that we needed for our homes and for our families." Old Navy, owned by Gap Inc , manufactures the vast majority of its clothing overseas, notably Vietnam, China, Bangladesh, and India.
Article Summary: US Vice President JD Vance seized on his wife's bargain maternity dress to score a political point on federal spending. US Second Lady Usha Vance mocked a New York Times column that analysed the "political symbolism" of her pregnancy wardrobe. Vance shared a photo of a receipt showing his wife had bought a $50 Old Navy maternity dress for $8.75 and wrote: "America: meet your next director of the federal budget!"

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: Man who shot sweets vendor dead over 'denying him jalebis' ahead of other customers gets life imprisonment
Article Text:
A vendor and a customer can be seen arguing while other customers wait. (AI-generated image used for representational purpose)





NEW DELHI: A Delhi court has sentenced a man to life imprisonment, 12 years after he shot dead a sweets vendor for refusing to serve him 'jalebis' ahead of other customers waiting in a queue.The incident took place on February 18, 2014, at a sweet shop near Bangla Sahib Gurudwara. According to the prosecution, when the vendor refused to let him skip the queue, the man slapped him, took out a pistol and fired at his head from close range.Additional sessions judge Dhirendra Rana was hearing arguments on the sentence of Neeraj, who was convicted earlier this month under IPC Section 302 (murder) and the Arms Act for unauthorised use of firearms.In an order dated June 8, the judge said, "Perusal of the record narrates that the convict entered into a quarrel with the deceased as he wanted that jalebis would be given to him before other customers, who were standing in the queue."When the deceased (vendor) refused the same, the convict slapped him and took out a pistol and fired a gunshot from close range on the head of the deceased," he added.The judge noted that the vendor was taken to a hospital, where he died, and that Neeraj was apprehended near the spot.A fake arms licence was also recovered from him."I have considered the mitigating circumstances involved in this case. Convict is not involved in any other case. He remained in custody throughout the trial. He is not a threat to society, and there are chances of his reformation," the judge said.He further said, "Therefore, the present case is not covered under the category of rarest of rare cases. Convict has been facing a protracted trial since 2014 and has a family containing his wife and two sons."He then sentenced the convict to life imprisonment for murder.(With inputs from PTI)
Article Summary: The incident took place on February 18, 2014, at a sweet shop near Bangla Sahib Gurudwara. Neeraj was convicted earlier this month under IPC Section 302 (murder) and the Arms Act for unauthorised use of firearms.

Article Title: Delhi hit-and-run: Techie was driving Thar that killed biker 2 days ago, arrested
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Two days after a 34-year-old biker Sarthak Mattoo was allegedly run over by a Thar at southwest Delhi’s Rajokri Flyover, police have apprehended the driver of the vehicle. Police said the Karnataka-registered vehicle belonged to a private company based in Bengaluru.The SUV had been leased to Sagar Saha from Bihar, who is employed with the company and posted in Bengaluru. At the time of the accident Thursday morning, Saha told police, his friend Apurv Singh was test-driving the Thar from Gurgaon to Dwarka Expressway and back. Saha was in the front passenger seat with Singh.Singh, 30, a software tester from Ghaziabad who lives in Gurgaon, was traced and apprehended. Police said it is unclear if he was intoxicated when he ran over 34-year-old Mattoo around 6.30am Thursday. Singh’s blood samples have been sent for tests.Sarthak’s life cut short two days before b’dayPolice said Saha and Singh were contacted after obtaining the ownership details of the vehicle and they joined the investigation.“There is no information to suggest that they were planning to flee or attempting to conceal evidence,” a senior police officer said.The vehicle has been seized.Sarthak, who worked with an event management company, was on his way to an assignment in Noida from his Gurgaon home at the time of the accident. The SUV hit Sarthak from behind before making a sharp cut, causing him to skid and come under its wheels. He was to turn 34 on Saturday.Surender Mattoo told media that a passerby had found his son lying on the flyover on the Delhi-Gurgaon expressway and contacted the last number called, which was his office.“Once the car ran him over, they could have taken him to the hospital,” Surender said, showing the scratched helmet that his son was reportedly wearing at the time of the accident. Police rushed Sarthak to a private hospital in Vasant Kunj, but he could not be saved.Surender further questioned the reliability of the blood test two days after the accident. “It is Sarthak’s birthday today. This was not an accident. It was a hit-and-run, an act of killing a man,” he said, demanding strict police action.
Article Summary: Sarthak Mattoo, 34, was allegedly run over by a Thar at southwest Delhi’s Rajokri Flyover. Police said the Karnataka-registered vehicle belonged to a private company based in Bengaluru. The SUV had been leased to Sagar Saha from Bihar, who is employed with the company.

Article Title: ‘Classic case of over-implication': Delhi court dismisses plea to revive dowry case
Article Text:
Photo for representation

New Delhi:





Refusing to revive criminal proceedings against a woman’s former husband and his relatives in a dowry harassment case, a Delhi court held that the allegations were “completely bereft of any specific dates or distinct incidents” and amounted to sweeping, generalised accusations.Calling the implication of the husband’s entire family despite them living separately a “classic case of over-implication in a matrimonial dispute”, additional sessions judge Harguravinder Singh Jaggi observed that forcing individuals to face trial on vague allegations would amount to an abuse of the legal process.The complainant had alleged that she was subjected to physical and mental torture, abuse, beatings and dowry demands by her husband, mother-in-law, father-in-law, sister-in-law and brother-in-law. She further claimed that her mother-in-law took possession of all her ancestral jewellery and never returned it. The woman also alleged that she was treated like a servant and was frequently locked in a room without food or water.However, the sessions court noted that the trial court had dismissed these allegations, describing them as “omnibus despite material statements on record indicating individual roles.”The defence argued that the complaint was vague and did not disclose any specific demand for dowry. It further contended that the case had been filed in Delhi to harass the accused, who are residents of Rajasthan , even though the couple’s divorce had already been finalised by a Jaipur court.
Article Summary: A Delhi court refused to revive criminal proceedings against a woman’s former husband and his relatives in a dowry harassment case. The complainant had alleged that she was subjected to physical and mental torture, abuse, beatings and dowry demands. The defence argued that the complaint was vague and did not disclose any specific demand for dowry.

Article Title: Jalna police bust honey-trap gang, woman kingpin among six arrested
Article Text:
The six accused in police custody

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

TOI





Jalna police have arrested six people, including an alleged woman kingpin with a history of honey-trap offences, for abducting a man, assaulting him and robbing him of cash and mobile phone.The complainant was also forced to transfer money through UPI after the accused recorded an obscene video. The accused also tried to extort more money from the relatives and friends of the complainant.According to the police, the woman kingpin, Sapna Santosh Nikalje (23), is a habitual offender with multiple honey-trap cases registered against her in Jalna and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar districts.Police alleged that her modus operandi involved luring victims, recording obscene photographs or videos, threatening to make them public, taking them to isolated places or lodges, summoning accomplices, assaulting the victims and robbing them. In an earlier case registered at Karmad police station, the victim allegedly died by suicide fearing social stigma.Following the detection, Jalna superintendent of police (SP) Teghbir Singh Sandhu appealed to the citizens who may have fallen prey to similar crimes to approach the police without fear.The latest incident took place on the night of June 20 under the jurisdiction of Chandanzira police station. According to police, the victim was intercepted on New Mondha Road and taken towards the Dry Port area.After the victim lodged a complaint on June 23, a case was registered at Chandanzira police station. Taking serious note of the offence, Sandhu and additional superintendent of police Vikram Sali directed police inspector Balasaheb Pawar to crack the case at the earliest.Sali, while talking to, said, “The coordinated efforts of the investigation team led to the arrest of all the accused within four hours of identifying the suspects and the recovery of crucial evidence.”The remaining five accused have been identified as Avinash Ganesh Zhote (26), Arjun Chaman Bahadur Rajput (34), Shankar Manik Jadhav (26), Atish Satish Gaikwad (19) and Sachin Laxman Kanthale (32).
Article Summary: Jalna police have arrested six people, including a woman kingpin, for alleged honey-trap offences. Police alleged that her modus operandi involved luring victims, recording obscene photographs or videos, taking them to isolated places or lodges, summoning accomplices and robbing them.
